title: Xtra Briny Mediterranean Smoked Turkey Neck Tagine
description: A savory and satisfying Mediterranean dish featuring smoked turkey neck, this tagine is perfect for a midnight craving, with its briny and flaky texture, and is satvik-friendly. It is made by emulsifying the ingredients to create a rich and creamy sauce.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the smoked turkey neck and cook until it is browned on all sides.
Add the lemon juice, chickpeas, cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer, then reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, for 2 hours, or until the turkey is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Remove the turkey neck from the pot and let it cool, then shred the meat and return it to the pot.
Simmer the tagine for an additional 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce has emulsified and the flavors have melded together.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or cilantro, if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
